title: Routing bends too close to fan-out and crosses reserved label/fan-out areas
summary: Fiber strand routing begins immediately after the fan-out and also passes through the middle of fan-out/label regions instead of traveling a minimum distance into the open center routing zone before bending.
visually_observable_issues: Some strand routes bend immediately near the fan-out instead of first traveling into the center routing zone.
A vertical routed segment passes through the strand abbreviation and OS circuit label area.
Routing appears to occur inside or through fan-out geometry rather than only in the open center of the diagram.
The routed paths are visually close to labels and fan-out exits, making the route hard to distinguish from fan-out geometry.

broken_requirements: Routes must remain inside the center routing zone.
Route segments and bends must avoid labels, fan-out geometry, cable bodies, and buffer tubes.
Left/right strands must travel at least the default 60px into the routing zone before a vertical bend.
Any bend point that violates minimum bend clearance fails this rule.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The image shows bends and routed segments starting too close to the fan-out and occupying reserved fan-out/label areas instead of staying in the open center routing zone.

broken_requirements: The fan-out does not perform center routing.
The routing engine must not bend inside the fan-out area.
The routing engine must not route through the fan-out area after leaving it.
Labels must avoid route lines and stay outside the routing zone.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The image shows route geometry crossing the fan-out/label area after the strand exits, so the fan-out is not preserved as a clean transition area.

broken_requirements: Fan-out spacing must support strand abbreviation labels, OS circuit labels, and clean entry into the routing zone.
Individual strand routes must preserve spacing around fan-out exits and bends.
Bends that are too close together or too close to reserved content fail readability.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The route placement crowds labels, fan-out exits, and bend points, reducing readability and clean traceability.

likely_broken_rules.rule_name: Fiber Strand Overlap, Crossing, and Collision
broken_requirements: Routes must remain visually traceable from fan-out exit to fusion splice dot.
Routes must avoid unnecessary crossing and route interference.
A path fails when it makes the splice relationship unclear or ignores an available cleaner path.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The crowding through the fan-out/label region creates route interference and makes the strand path less traceable than a center-zone route.

notes: This should be treated as a hard bad example because the defect is structural, not only aesthetic.

Expected correction: each strand should leave its fan-out cleanly, travel into the open center routing zone by at least the configured bend-clearance distance, then bend or transition lanes only inside that center zone.
Routing should never be drawn on top of or through fiber strand abbreviations, OS circuit labels, fiber fan-out geometry, buffer tubes, fiber cable bodies, or outside the diagram.
